title: sedum rosea subsp. arcticum
intro: Sedum rosea subsp. arcticum, also known as Arctic Stonecrop, is a perennial succulent plant species native to the Arctic tundra regions. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and other health issues. Sedum rosea subsp. arcticum can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used topically in the form of salves and ointments for its medicinal properties.
